svg.title: The Clock face across the four supported sizes
svg.desc: The digital Clock rendered at each of its supported size classes. Small is a one-by-one glance showing just the time with no header and no date. Medium is the default two-by-one card showing the time over a date line. Wide is a three-by-one banner showing a second-clock example: a New York zone kicker, the time, and the date, because a zone label appears only when a timezone override is set. Large is the two-by-two wall-dominating hero with the time in the largest display step over the date. A panel lists the per-size time type step, the date-line treatment, the second-clock zone-label rule that derives a human label from the IANA id, and that v1 is digital with the analog face deferred.
svg.text: Clock face: across small / medium / wide / large
Digital, v1. Self-ticks from the device clock; tabular figures so digits don’t jitter. The host draws no chrome on the (always) Fresh state.
Clock face: digital (v1). Analog face deferred (§10).
Per-size time step: small 34 · medium 56 · wide 64 · large 96
Date line: below the time, textMuted, per dateFormat.
Hidden at small, or when showDate = false.
Zone label (second clock): shown only with a timezone
override. IANA id → human label: America/New_York → “New York”.
A device-local clock shows no kicker.
Night: deep-red useAmbient palette (see the ambient mockup).
